rqs: Which of Quick Sort, Merge Sort, Heap Sort, Radix Sort, or Tim Sort offers the best trade‑off between time complexity and stability versus classic sorts in academic journals?
In which scenarios do software developers and data engineers prefer Tim Sort over Insertion Sort based on comparisons and swaps reported in open source repositories?
What is the impact of choosing Quick Sort versus Counting Sort on memory usage for embedded systems as documented in industry reports?
base_query: ("Software Developers" OR "Algorithm Researchers" OR "Data Engineers" OR "Computer Science Students" OR "Embedded Systems" OR "researcher") AND ("Quick Sort" OR "Merge Sort" OR "Heap Sort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "Tim Sort") AND ("Insertion Sort" OR "Selection Sort" OR "Bubble Sort" OR "Shell Sort" OR "Counting Sort") AND ("Time Complexity" OR "Space Complexity" OR "Stability" OR "Comparisons" OR "Swaps") AND ("Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Open Source Repositories" OR "Benchmark Suites" OR "Industry Reports")
arxiv_query: (all:"Software Developers" OR all:"Algorithm Researchers" OR all:"Data Engineers" OR all:"Computer Science Students" OR all:"Embedded Systems" OR all:"researcher") AND (all:"Quick Sort" OR all:"Merge Sort" OR all:"Heap Sort" OR all:"Radix Sort" OR all:"Tim Sort") AND (all:"Insertion Sort" OR all:"Selection Sort" OR all:"Bubble Sort" OR all:"Shell Sort" OR all:"Counting Sort") AND (all:"Time Complexity" OR all:"Space Complexity" OR all:"Stability" OR all:"Comparisons" OR all:"Swaps") AND (all:"Academic Journals" OR all:"Conference Proceedings" OR all:"Open Source Repositories" OR all:"Benchmark Suites" OR all:"Industry Reports")

questions.text: Is the algorithm implementation (pseudocode or code) fully described?
Are experimental settings (hardware, datasets, benchmarks) clearly reported?
Does the study use validated performance metrics and statistical analysis?
Is the source of data or benchmark suite cited and publicly accessible?
Are results compared against baseline classic sorts (e.g., Insertion, Bubble)?
